description: Lock interstellar_travel to source-backed interstellar-space and star-system travel concepts, avoiding unsupported colonization, AGI, or biomedical-longevity prerequisites.

checks.reason: NASA NIAC treats fusion propulsion as a plausible high-energy path for heliosphere and local-interstellar-medium missions, but not the only possible propulsion route.
NASA interstellar-medium mission studies require demanding heat-shield and thermal-protection materials for close-solar maneuvers.
Closed-loop life support applies to crewed long-duration interstellar travel concepts, while robotic interstellar probes do not require it.
Robotic interstellar-space probes and precursor missions do not require prior off-world settlement.
NASA interstellar-probe studies emphasize propulsion, trajectory, materials, and operations rather than AGI as a prerequisite.
Biomedical longevity escape velocity is not required for robotic interstellar probes and is not the direct crew-support technology for long-duration human spaceflight.
